questions.q: Raja Ramanna is best remembered for leading the team behind which milestone event?
options: India's first space mission to the Moon
questions.explanation: Ramanna led the team that carried out India's first nuclear test in 1974.

questions.q: In which scientific field did Raja Ramanna primarily work?
questions.explanation: Ramanna was a nuclear physicist who specialised in nuclear fission and reactor research.

questions.q: What was the codename of India's first nuclear test that Ramanna helped lead?
questions.explanation: India's first nuclear test in 1974 was codenamed 'Smiling Buddha'.

questions.q: In which year did India conduct its first nuclear test under Ramanna's leadership?
questions.explanation: The Smiling Buddha test was conducted on 18 May 1974.

questions.q: At which site in Rajasthan was India's 1974 nuclear test conducted?
questions.explanation: The 1974 test was carried out at the Pokhran test range in Rajasthan.

questions.q: In which country was Raja Ramanna born?
questions.explanation: Raja Ramanna was born in India, in Tumkur, in the present-day state of Karnataka.

questions.q: Besides being a physicist, Raja Ramanna was also an accomplished performer of which art?
questions.explanation: Ramanna was a talented pianist and a noted lover of Western classical music.

questions.q: Raja Ramanna spent much of his career at which Indian nuclear research centre at Trombay?
questions.explanation: Ramanna worked at and later directed the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) at Trombay.

questions.q: The 1974 Pokhran test was officially described by the Indian government as what kind of explosion?
questions.explanation: India officially described the 1974 test as a 'peaceful nuclear explosion'.

questions.q: Which prominent earlier Indian physicist mentored Ramanna and built India's atomic energy programme?
questions.explanation: Homi Bhabha, founder of India's nuclear programme, recruited and mentored Ramanna at TIFR and Trombay.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Raja Ramanna born in 1925?
questions.explanation: Ramanna was born on 28 January 1925 in Tumkur, Karnataka.

questions.q: Ramanna earned his doctorate in physics from which university?
questions.explanation: Ramanna received his PhD in nuclear physics from King's College London in 1949.

questions.q: Which civilian government post did Ramanna briefly hold in the late 1980s?
questions.explanation: Ramanna served as India's Minister of State for Defence in 1990.

questions.q: Ramanna served as Director of which institution for several years during his career?
questions.explanation: Ramanna was Director of BARC, a role central to India's nuclear research efforts.

questions.q: Which of India's highest civilian honours was conferred on Raja Ramanna?
questions.explanation: Ramanna was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honour, in 1975.

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister of India at the time of the 1974 Pokhran nuclear test?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was Prime Minister and authorised the 1974 Smiling Buddha test.

questions.q: Which fundamental nuclear process is central to the device tested at Pokhran in 1974?
questions.explanation: The 1974 device was a fission bomb, relying on the splitting of heavy nuclei such as plutonium.

questions.q: Ramanna later served as the founding head of which advanced studies institution in Bengaluru?
questions.explanation: Ramanna was the founding director of the National Institute of Advanced Studies (NIAS) in Bangalore.

questions.q: Ramanna's early reactor work at Trombay centred on India's first research reactor, named what?
questions.explanation: Apsara, commissioned in 1956, was Asia's first research reactor, and Ramanna contributed to its development.

questions.q: Which fissile material was the core of the device used in India's 1974 nuclear test?
questions.explanation: The 1974 Smiling Buddha device used plutonium produced in India's reactors as its fissile core.

questions.q: Raja Ramanna also held a seat in which house of the Indian Parliament?
questions.explanation: Ramanna was nominated as a member of the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of Parliament.

questions.q: Ramanna's autobiography reflecting on science and his life was titled what?
questions.explanation: Ramanna's autobiography is titled 'Years of Pilgrimage'.

questions.q: Roughly what was the officially stated yield of India's 1974 Pokhran nuclear test?
questions.explanation: India officially stated a yield of about 12 kilotons of TNT for the 1974 test, though independent estimates varied.

questions.q: The plutonium for the 1974 test was produced using which reactor at Trombay?
questions.explanation: Plutonium for Smiling Buddha was reprocessed from spent fuel of the Canada-supplied Cirus reactor.

questions.q: On what exact date was India's first nuclear test conducted?
questions.explanation: The Smiling Buddha test took place on 18 May 1974, coinciding with Buddha Purnima.

questions.q: Which early national science prize did Ramanna win in 1963 for his research?
questions.explanation: Ramanna received the Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize for Science and Technology in 1963 for his work in physics.

questions.q: Before joining the atomic energy establishment, Ramanna's doctoral research focused on which area of physics?
questions.explanation: Ramanna's PhD work in London was in nuclear physics, the field that defined his later career.

questions.q: Ramanna chaired which national advisory body on atomic energy during his career?
options: The Atomic Energy Commission of India
questions.explanation: Ramanna served as Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission of India.

questions.q: In what year did Raja Ramanna pass away?
questions.explanation: Raja Ramanna died on 24 September 2004.

questions.q: The team that built the 1974 device kept it deliberately small; what colloquial name did insiders give the assembled device?
questions.explanation: The device and the test alike were nicknamed 'Smiling Buddha', the codename by which the operation is remembered.
